name: Halloween
description: Horror movies, monsters, and Halloween traditions

questions.q: Mary Shelley is the author of which classic horror novel, published in 1818?
choices: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde

source.notes: Full title: Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus.

questions.q: In what year was the first 'Halloween' slasher film released?

source.notes: Directed by John Carpenter. Starred Jamie Lee Curtis in her film debut.

questions.q: In the 'Halloween' film series, what is the name of the masked killer?

source.notes: His mask is a repurposed Captain Kirk mask painted white.

questions.q: Which actress has played Laurie Strode in the 'Halloween' franchise since 1978?

source.notes: Daughter of 'Psycho' star Janet Leigh. 'Scream Queen' icon.

questions.q: Which type of supernatural creature is Count Dracula?

source.notes: Bram Stoker's novel published 1897. Inspired by Vlad the Impaler.

questions.q: Which actor is best known as the original Boris Karloff role: the monster in 1931's 'Frankenstein'?

source.notes: Born William Henry Pratt. Reprised role in 'Bride of Frankenstein' (1935).

questions.q: In 'The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde,' what was the doctor's first name?

source.notes: Dr. Henry Jekyll. Novella by Robert Louis Stevenson, 1886.

questions.q: Carrie, the novel adapted into a famous horror film, was written by which American author?

source.notes: King's first published novel (1974). 1976 film directed by Brian De Palma.

questions.q: 'The Shining' is set at which fictional haunted hotel?

source.notes: King based it on the real Stanley Hotel in Colorado.

questions.q: In the original 'Halloween' film, how old was Michael Myers when he murdered his sister?

source.notes: Shown in the opening scene on Halloween night, 1963 in Haddonfield, Illinois.

questions.q: The 1999 horror hit 'The Blair Witch Project' is set in the woods of which U.S. state?

source.notes: Set in Burkittsville, MD. Pioneered the 'found footage' genre.

questions.q: Jack-o'-lanterns are traditionally carved from what?

source.notes: Originally carved from turnips in Ireland; pumpkins adopted by Irish immigrants in America.

questions.q: How many eyes does a Cyclops have?

source.notes: From Greek mythology. Polyphemus is the most famous, blinded by Odysseus.

questions.q: How many bones does the average adult human skeleton contain?

source.notes: Babies are born with ~270, many fuse together during development.

questions.q: In the Tim Burton film 'The Nightmare Before Christmas,' what town does Jack Skellington rule over?

source.notes: Jack is the Pumpkin King. Directed by Henry Selick, produced by Tim Burton.

questions.q: In Charles M. Schulz's 'It's the Great Pumpkin, Charlie Brown,' what does Charlie Brown receive in his trick-or-treat bag?

source.notes: First aired 1966. 'I got a rock' is one of Charlie Brown's most famous lines.

questions.q: Michael Jackson's iconic 'Thriller' music video and album were released in which year?

source.notes: Album: Nov 30, 1982. 'Thriller' video: Dec 2, 1983. Directed by John Landis.

questions.q: The Old English word 'hallow,' from which 'Halloween' derives, means what?

source.notes: 'Halloween' = 'All Hallows' Eve' = eve of All Saints' Day (November 1).

questions.q: Which actor played the monster hunter Gabriel Van Helsing in the 2004 film 'Van Helsing'?

source.notes: Directed by Stephen Sommers. Kate Beckinsale co-starred.

questions.q: In a botanical sense, what category of food does a pumpkin fall under?

source.notes: Botanically a berry (specifically a pepo). Contains seeds and develops from flower.
